name: Vegetable Quinoa Paniyaram
description: A fermented tiffin that stays light on the stomach while delivering enough fiber to keep breakfast steady. Vegetable Quinoa Paniyaram brings a distinct structure, seasoning pattern, and grain-legume balance to the table.
cooking_steps: Heat the paniyaram pan over medium heat for 2 minutes and brush each cavity lightly with oil.
Mix onion, vegetable, ginger, chilli, curry leaves, and salt into the batter. Fill each cavity three-quarters full.
Cook covered on medium-low heat for 3 minutes until the edges set and the bottom turns deep golden.
Turn each paniyaram with a skewer, cook 2 more minutes until evenly browned and springy, then serve hot.
meal_prep_notes: Paniyaram keeps 2 days in the fridge. Reheat in the same mould pan over low heat for 3 minutes or steam briefly. For batch prep, ferment the batter earlier and mix in vegetables only just before cooking.
